page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs derived from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 512 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance via stress-ng, alongside high memory write bandwidth and small-block read bandwidth. Passmark tests show strong capabilities in database operations, encryption, and physics calculations. Redis SET operations reach 63.0 million operations per second. While ray tracing performance is strong, other tasks such as software compilation, image processing, and text processing fall within average ranges. Large-block uncached memory reads and static web serving throughput also register average performance.

This server configuration offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of 512 GB RAM and strong database operation benchmarks positions this instance for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 physical cores. This configuration lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory write bandwidth, and strong database operations, with Redis SET throughput reaching 63.0 million operations per second. Conversely, large-block uncached memory reads and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density per core, making it a cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC CPU. It delivers strong database operations and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs and five hundred twelve gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory write bandwidth
Delivers 63.0 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
